spec_match.rationale: Uses `::column` on a multi-column text container, lets the user toggle background, border, padding, box-shadow — all post-layout properties the spec allows.

interactive.rationale: Seven live controls (range, color, select) drive a dynamically-injected stylesheet via `<style id='columnStyleEl'>` and a generated-CSS readout. The pseudo updates on every input event.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index blurb promises background, border, padding, box-shadow on a live multicol text layout with generated CSS — exactly what's delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Allows padding 0-24px but doesn't explain that padding on `::column` inflates the column box (post-layout) — at padding 24 with count 5 watch text overflow. The probe state says `CSS applied anyway` for unsupported browsers but no fallback rendering shown.

references.rationale: No `see also` or `references` section. The chromestatus.com/feature/5192332683771904 link is missing from the page — required for the cleanup script.

design_system.rationale: Page chrome uses CSS variables, but the box-shadow select options use rgba(0,0,0,0.1) literals that would be more legible as design-system tokens. Color inputs default to raw hex (acceptable since user-controlled).

openQuestions.title: Add a references section with the chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: Page has no see-also block. Required by CLAUDE.md — the cleanup script needs the chromestatus.com/feature/5192332683771904 link to recover the folder slug. Add a section with the link, CSS Overflow Level 5 spec, and links to sibling concepts.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the padding overflow case
openQuestions.detail: Padding on `::column` is a post-layout property — it inflates the column box without re-flowing text. At padding=24 with count=5, columns will overlap or text will clip. Add a visible warning when padding × (count+1) > available width.

openQuestions.title: Add a `try a forbidden property` row
openQuestions.detail: Spec allows only post-layout properties. Add an extra row that exposes `width`, `margin`, `font-size` so authors can see the browser silently ignores them. Most important constraint and currently invisible.

openQuestions.title: Preset themes
openQuestions.detail: Add 3-4 preset buttons (newspaper, magazine, code-side-by-side, gallery) that snap all sliders to a coherent look. Lowers activation energy for authors who want a starting point.

summary: Most complete authoring tool of the five concepts — seven live controls and a dynamic stylesheet. Two real misses: no references section (chromestatus link missing), and no teaching of which CSS properties the pseudo silently ignores.
